Dear Ms. Crouch: 
Thank you for your request for information on the filing of 
Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) by the consultants 
who work for the Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority. 
Specifically, the Authority contracted with Jones and Stokes 
Associates, Inc., an environmental consulting firm, for the 
preparation of an Environmental Impact Report. 
The company 
indicated that it does not feel that the employees who work on 
the project should be required to complete Statements. 
You 
asked for guidance in determining when these, and other 
consultants, must file Statements. 
The Political Reform Act requires a consultant to a 
~ocal 
government entity to be designated in the entity!s code if the 
consultant makes or participates in the making of decisions 
which may foreseably have a material effect on any financial 
interests. 
(Government Code Section 873021:./.) 
A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any 
natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice, 
recommenda tion or counsel to a local government agency ... II 
(2 Cal. 
Adm. Co de Section 18700 (a) (2) I copy enclosed). 
Th i s teL'm does not 
include, however, a person who: 
(A) 
Conducts research and arrives at 
conclusions with respest to his or her rendition 
of information, advice, recommendation or counsel 
!/ Hereinafter, all statutory references made are to 
the Government Code unless other~ise specified. 


Ms. Carolyn S. Crouch 
March 22, 1983 
Page Two 
independent of the control and direction of the 
agency or any agency official, other than normal 
contract monitoring; and 
(B) 
Possesses no authority with respect to 
any agency decision beyond the rendition of 
information, advice, recommendation or counsel. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18700 (a) (2) (A) and (B). 
When a person is hired under a contract to perform a 
specific task, such as the preparation of an EIR, he or she 
ordinarily comes within the exemption. 
Under such circum-
stances, the individual uses his or her expertise to render 
professional services according to the specifications of a 
contract, and his or her judgment is not subject to review 
or direction by the Authority. 
Essentially, the individual 
is being called upon to deliver a finished product, not to 
participate in the governmental decisions made by the 
Authority. 
Applying this analysis to what you have told us 
of the contract with Jones and Stokes Associates, it does 
not appear that the individuals who will be working on the 
EIR must file Statements.ll 
On 
th~ other hand, if the Authority contracts with 
consultants who do not satisfy all the criteria for the 
exemption, they must be designated. 
This determination 
must be made on a contract-by-contract basis. 
If you have 
a question regarding a specific contract, please feel free 
to contact our office for advice. 
I hope this information will be helpful to you. 
Thank 
you for your concern about this matter.

is included as Attachment I.

pare the Final EIR for a cost not to exceed $700; this estimate

alignment.

be necessary.

Corporate Background 
Jones & Stokes Associates, Inc. was formed in 1970 to 
meet the growing need for responsible,scientific, environ-
mental expertise. 
The firm maintains a professional staff of 
approximately 25 persons offering diversified experience in 
such.disciplines as terrestrial and aquatic ecology, zoology, 
botany, wildlife management, fisheries biology, marine biology, 
envirolliuental engineering, water resources management, range 
management, forestry, geography, urban and regional planning, 
resource economics, recreational planning, air quality and 
noise analysis, and photo interpretation. 
Jones & Stokes Associates management and staff have 
broad experience working with legislative committees and many 
federal, state and local governmental agencies in natural 
resources management. 
Staff experience in the EIR/EIS process 
includes public agency and academic perspectives as well as 
the private consultant role. 
Involvement with citizen 
conservation groups over many years has provided Jones & Stokes 
Associates with insight into the goals, interests and concerns 
of conservation groups regarding the management of natural 
resources and maintenance of environmental quality. 
Members 
of the firm also have presented expert testimony in various 
legal and administrative proceedings, including those of the 
California Energy Commission, State Water Resources Control 
Board, and California Public utilities Commission. 
Members of the firm have extensive experience in 
organizing and managing interdisciplinary teams.

frequently establishes teams through subcontracts for special 
studies. 
Jones & Stokes also serves as a subcontractor to 
other firms.

and is a "mandatory employer" with special employment 
consideration given to Vietnam War veterans, and handicapped

sections.question_synthetic: Must consultants working for a local government entity on specific projects like Environmental Impact Reports file Statements of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Consultants hired to perform specific technical tasks like preparing an EIR are generally exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests if they conduct work independently and have no authority over agency decisions. However, this determination must be made on a contract-by-contract basis depending on whether the consultant satisfies all exemption criteria.

Crouch Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority 2060 Chicago Avenue, Suite C-3 Riverside, CA 92507 Enforcement 322-6441 Statements of Economic Interest 322-6444 Re: Our Advice No. A-83-069 Dear Ms. Crouch: Thank you for your request for information on the filing of Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) by the consultants who work for the Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority. Specifically, the Authority contracted with Jones and Stokes Associates, Inc., an environmental consulting firm, for the preparation of an Environmental Impact Report. The company indicated that it does not feel that the employees who work on the project should be required to complete Statements. You asked for guidance in determining when these, and other consultants, must file Statements. The Political Reform Act requires a consultant to a ~ocal government entity to be designated in the entity!s code if the consultant makes or participates in the making of decisions which may foreseably have a material effect on any financial interests. (Government Code Section 873021:./.) A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice, recommenda tion or counsel to a local government agency ... II (2 Cal. Adm. Co de Section 18700 (a) (2) I copy enclosed). Th i s teL'm does not include, however, a person who: (A) Conducts research and arrives at conclusions with respest to his or her rendition of information, advice, recommendation or counsel !/ Hereinafter, all statutory references made are to the Government Code unless other~ise specified. Ms. Carolyn S. Crouch March 22, 1983 Page Two independent of the control and direction of the agency or any agency official, other than normal contract monitoring; and (B) Possesses no authority with respect to any agency decision beyond the rendition of information, advice, recommendation or counsel. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 (a) (2) (A) and (B). When a person is hired under a contract to perform a specific task, such as the preparation of an EIR, he or she ordinarily comes within the exemption. Under such circum- stances, the individual uses his or her expertise to render professional services according to the specifications of a contract, and his or her judgment is not subject to review or direction by the Authority. Essentially, the individual is being called upon to deliver a finished product, not to participate in the governmental decisions made by the Authority. Applying this analysis to what you have told us of the contract with Jones and Stokes Associates, it does not appear that the individuals who will be working on the EIR must file Statements.ll On th~ other hand, if the Authority contracts with consultants who do not satisfy all the criteria for the exemption, they must be designated. This determination must be made on a contract-by-contract basis. If you have a question regarding a specific contract, please feel free
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority's inquiry about whether consultants, specifically Jones and Stokes Associates employees working on an EIR, must file Statements of Economic Interests. The FPPC concluded that consultants performing specific contracted tasks are generally exempt from filing requirements if they work independently and lack decision-making authority.
